_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-30R Ketorolac (Revised 12/10/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2026). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-30R: Ketorolac — MDHHS Approved 1/5/2026, Reviewed 2026
contraindications: Active labor or women who are breastfeeding
Bleeding or high risk of bleeding
precautions: Maximum treatment duration is 5 days (hospital and field combined) — EMS use is typically single dose
Avoid in elderly patients with renal insufficiency
Concurrent use with other NSAIDs increases GI and renal risk
Pediatric use: >5 years only per Michigan protocol
expected_effects: Analgesic effect comparable to opioids for certain pain types (renal colic, musculoskeletal)
Onset within 30-60 minutes IM; faster IV
notes: Adult dose: 15 mg IM or IV (Michigan protocol — lower than some national guidelines to reduce adverse effects)
Pediatric >5 years: 0.5 mg/kg IM or IV; maximum 15 mg
Single-dose EMS use only in most cases
Good alternative to opioids when NSAID is not contraindicated

dosing.dose: 0.5 mg/kg IM or IV; maximum 15 mg
